info.title: Kharko Dozor REST API
info.description: Public SDK ingest endpoint (`/api/ingest`) plus the dashboard's internal API (session-cookie authenticated). Generated from the repo's zod schemas; committed for PR-time diffing.

post.summary: Submit a batch of rrweb events from the SDK
post.description: Public-key authenticated. Always returns 204 on success. Accepts gzipped payloads via Content-Encoding: gzip.

parameters.name: x-dozor-public-key
parameters.description: Project public key (dp_<32hex>) — created in Settings → Organizations → Project.

metadata.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 14_4) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/17.4 Safari/605.1.15

options.description: Always returns 204 with CORS headers. No authentication required — browsers send this before the POST.

get.summary: List projects across the caller's organisations
get.description: Returns every project in every org the caller belongs to. Optional `?organizationId=` narrows to one org. Keys are masked — plaintext only via `GET /api/projects/{id}/key`.

post.description: OWNER-only. Mints a fresh API key for the new project.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER of the target organisation

patch.description: ADMIN+ — metadata only, not key lifecycle.

403.description: Caller is not ADMIN+ of the project's organisation

delete.description: OWNER-only. Cascades sessions → slices → events → tracked users. The deletion is permanent — no soft-delete tombstone.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER

get.summary: Reveal a project's plaintext API key
get.description: OWNER-only. The single endpoint that returns plaintext key material; called from the dashboard's copy button on click. Sets `Cache-Control: no-store` so no proxy caches the value.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER

post.description: OWNER-only. The old key stops working immediately — no grace window. The response carries the new plaintext key for one-time copy.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER

patch.summary: Set the project's default display-name trait key
patch.description: ADMIN+. The trait key is consulted as step 3 of the 4-step display-name resolution (see `resolveDisplayName`). Pass `null` in `traitKey` to reset.

403.description: Caller is not ADMIN+

get.description: Returns every org the caller is a member of, ordered by creation date ascending — Personal Space (always the oldest) surfaces first. Each entry carries the caller's role + the org's member count.

post.description: Creates a TEAM-type org with the caller as its OWNER. Personal Space is auto-provisioned by Auth.js's `createUser` event — this route only mints TEAM orgs.

patch.description: ADMIN+. `regenerateAvatar: true` rolls the DiceBear seed to a new UUID. Both fields optional; an empty body is a no-op `204`.

204.description: Organisation updated (or no-op for empty body)

403.description: Caller is not ADMIN+

delete.description: OWNER-only. Cascades projects → sessions → slices → events → tracked users. Personal Space deletion is rejected with `403`. Members whose `activeOrganizationId` pointed here are flipped to their Personal Space first.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER, or attempting to delete Personal Space

patch.description: Persists `User.activeOrganizationId` (mirrored to JWT). Membership is verified inside the same transaction so the active-org pointer can never flip to an org the caller doesn't belong to.

403.description: Caller is not a member of the target organisation

get.description: VIEWER+ — every member sees the full roster. Role-change / remove are gated tighter on the `[memberId]` routes.

403.description: Caller is not a member of the organisation

patch.description: OWNER-only. Multiple OWNERs per org are allowed — single-OWNER lock-out is not enforced by the API.

parameters.name: memberId
parameters.description: Membership id (cuid) — NOT the user id

403.description: Caller is not OWNER

404.description: Member not found in this organisation

delete.description: Two shapes — self-leave (any role, including last OWNER which triggers ownership transfer to the next-oldest ADMIN) or remove-other (OWNER-only). Personal Space leave is rejected. Sole-member leave is rejected — the route surface answer is to delete the org instead.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER (when removing other), or attempting to leave Personal Space

409.description: Sole-member leave — delete the organisation instead

get.summary: List outstanding invites for the org
get.description: OWNER-only — invite lifecycle is governance. Past-TTL rows are filtered out lazily and flipped to `EXPIRED` in the background.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER

post.description: OWNER-only. Idempotent refresh-or-create: an existing PENDING invite to the same email is updated in place (TTL reset, role swapped, email re-fired). Email send is fire-and-forget — the invite row exists regardless of SMTP outcome. Per-sender rate-limit (`INVITE_DAILY_LIMIT = 100/day`) returns `429` when capped.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER, or org is Personal Space

409.description: Recipient is already a member — change their role instead

429.description: Daily invite-send cap reached for this sender

patch.description: OWNER-only. Discriminated body — `{ action: 'change-role', role }` updates the role, `{ action: 'extend' }` resets the TTL and re-attributes the inviter to the acting user.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER

404.description: Invite not found in this organisation, or not PENDING

delete.description: OWNER-only. Hard-delete (not a status flip) so the refresh-or-create path on `POST` keeps its idempotency without filtering revoked rows.

403.description: Caller is not OWNER

404.description: Invite not found in this organisation, or not PENDING

get.description: VIEWER+. Scoped to the caller's active org. Filters: `?search=` (matches `externalId`, case-insensitive), `?projectIds=id1,id2`, `?range=` preset (`today` / `7d` / `30d` / `all`), `?sort=date|duration`, `?sortDir=asc|desc`. Display name is resolved server-side via the 4-step chain.

400.description: No active organisation set on the caller

403.description: Caller is not a member of the active organisation

get.summary: Full session detail for the replay page
get.description: VIEWER+ of the owning org. Returns metadata + slice list. Events are loaded per-slice via the `/slices/{sliceIndex}/events` sibling endpoint. Legacy pre-slice sessions inline their full event stream so old recordings still play.

example.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 14_4) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/17.4 Safari/605.1.15

403.description: Caller is not a VIEWER+ of the owning organisation

delete.description: ADMIN+ (not OWNER) — QA / staging cleanup loops stay unblocked without an owner on call. Cascades slices and events.

403.description: Caller is not ADMIN+ of the owning organisation

get.summary: Stream rrweb events for a single slice
get.description: VIEWER+. Loaded on demand by the replay viewer as the user scrubs into a slice — avoids shipping the full event log upfront for long multi-slice sessions. Events are timestamp-ascending so the rrweb Replayer consumes them as a stream.

403.description: Caller is not a VIEWER+ of the owning organisation

get.summary: KPI aggregate for the sessions list stats strip
get.description: VIEWER+. Returns four KPIs scoped to the caller's active org: total sessions, total duration, average duration (rounded), active today (created in the last 24 h). Single SQL round-trip via `$queryRaw` with conditional aggregation.

400.description: No active organisation set on the caller

403.description: Caller is not a member of the active organisation

post.summary: Cancel a session — SDK `stop()` teardown path
post.description: Public-key authenticated. Hard-deletes the session row matching `(projectId, externalId)`. Quiet no-op when no row exists — cancellation can race with the first ingest batch arriving. The `(projectId, externalId)` scoping prevents one project's key from cancelling another project's session.

parameters.name: x-dozor-public-key
parameters.description: Project public key (dp_<32hex>) — created in Settings → Organizations → Project.

400.description: Body failed validation (sessionId not a UUID)

options.description: Always returns 204 with CORS headers. No authentication required.

get.description: VIEWER+. Scoped to the caller's active org. Filters: `?search=` (matches `externalId` OR `customName`, case-insensitive), `?projectIds=`, `?statuses=ONLINE,ACTIVE_24H,...`, `?sort=last-seen|sessions|active-time|newest`, `?sortDir=`. Status is derived (not indexable) — when status filter is active the route over-fetches up to 500 rows and applies it in JS.

400.description: No active organisation set on the caller

403.description: Caller is not a member of the active organisation

get.description: VIEWER+. Same shape `loadTrackedUserDetail` produces for the user-detail page Server Component prefetch — `HydrationBoundary` skips the on-mount refetch because the wire format is byte-identical.

403.description: Caller is not a VIEWER+ of the owning organisation

patch.description: ADMIN+. Body fields `customName` and `traitKey` are independently optional; omitted fields stay unchanged, `null` clears the field (falls back through resolver chain), `string` sets the override (trimmed + validated). Implements steps 1–2 of the 4-step resolver chain.

400.description: Body failed validation (or empty body — at least one field required)

403.description: Caller is not ADMIN+ of the owning organisation

get.description: VIEWER+. Three SQL queries in parallel — histogram, page distribution, KPI aggregates — in a rolling window picked from `?range=` (default `24h`). `Cache-Control: no-store` because the histogram advances with each ingest batch.

403.description: Caller is not a VIEWER+ of the owning organisation

get.summary: Sessions for one tracked user, cursor-paginated
get.description: VIEWER+. Slim shape — no event payload, no trait JSON. Detail fields hydrate per-row via the session detail endpoint on demand. `userDisplayName` is `null` here on purpose (the user-detail page already surfaces the identity above the table).

403.description: Caller is not a VIEWER+ of the owning organisation

get.description: VIEWER+. Lightweight — `MAX(Session.endedAt)` keyed on the indexed `trackedUserId`, no `Event` table scan. Online flag flips when the most recent session ended within `ONLINE_THRESHOLD_MS`. `Cache-Control: no-store` so polling sees fresh values.

403.description: Caller is not a VIEWER+ of the owning organisation

get.summary: Sessions timeline in a rolling window
get.description: VIEWER+. `?range=` matches the activity histogram (`6h` / `24h` / `7d`). Server computes `from = now - windowMs` itself — sending raw ISO from the client would shift the TanStack Query key on every render. Sessions overlap the window if they `startedAt ≤ to AND (endedAt ≥ from OR endedAt IS NULL)`.

403.description: Caller is not a VIEWER+ of the owning organisation

get.summary: KPI aggregate for the users list stats strip
get.description: VIEWER+. Four KPIs scoped to the caller's active org: total tracked users, online now, active in last 24 h, new this week. Single SQL round-trip via `$queryRaw` with conditional aggregation + a `LATERAL` join to resolve `MAX(Session.endedAt)` per user.

400.description: No active organisation set on the caller

403.description: Caller is not a member of the active organisation

get.description: Returns name + email + avatar + linked OAuth accounts + registered passkeys. Powers the settings page's connect / disconnect / rename affordances.

404.description: User row missing — should never happen post-auth

patch.description: Renames the signed-in user. Avatar regeneration has its own endpoint (`POST /api/user/avatar`) — the two actions live in different parts of the profile form.

delete.description: Destructive. Confirmation phrase enforced server-side via `deleteAccountSchema` so a misclick can't wipe the account. One transaction: solo-member orgs are deleted outright (cascades invites + full data hierarchy); shared orgs get ownership transfer so an ownerless org is impossible after the cascade. The user row deletes last — cascades accounts, authenticators, memberships.

post.description: Rolls the avatar seed to a fresh UUID. The `image` URL changes; everything else stays. Returns the new URL so the client updates its avatar slot without a full profile refetch.

patch.summary: Update the user's UI locale preference
patch.description: Validates against the canonical `LOCALES` tuple. The session refresh + URL prefix swap happen client-side: on `204` the client calls `session.update({})` to force a JWT refresh, then `router.replace(pathname, { locale })` to swap the URL. All three steps are sequenced inside `useUpdateLocaleMutation.onSuccess`.

delete.description: Only unlinks the local `Account` row — does NOT revoke the provider-side consent (users manage that in Google / GitHub settings). Last-login-method guard runs in a `Serializable` transaction: counts remaining OAuth accounts + passkeys + email-OTP availability AFTER the hypothetical unlink. If zero would remain, returns `409` and the row is preserved — prevents single-click lockout.

404.description: No such linked account for this user

409.description: Cannot unlink — would leave the user with zero sign-in methods

patch.description: Lets the user distinguish 'MacBook Touch ID' from 'iPhone Face ID' in the authenticators list. Scoped by `(credentialID, userId)` so a credentialID leak can't let another user rename the passkey.

404.description: Passkey not found for this user

delete.description: Same `(credentialID, userId)` scoping as rename. The account stays usable via other authenticators (OAuth, OTP) as long as at least one remains — there's no equivalent to the OAuth last-method guard here because passkey removal alone never strands a user.

404.description: Passkey not found for this user

get.summary: List pending invites addressed to the caller's email
get.description: Lazy expiry — past-TTL rows are filtered out and status-flipped to `EXPIRED` in the background. The daily cron hard-deletes past-TTL rows overnight; this keeps the schema clean between runs without an extra scheduled job.

post.description: Atomic transaction — `Membership` create + invite `status → ACCEPTED` in one round-trip so a crash between writes can't leave a dangling PENDING row for someone who already has membership. Returns the org id + name so the client can navigate to it without a profile refetch.

403.description: Invite email doesn't match the caller's email

404.description: Invite not found, expired, or no longer PENDING

post.description: Hard-delete (not a `DECLINED` status flip) — declined invites carry no audit value (admin-side list renders only PENDING rows) and the extra enum would buy nothing. If an admin still wants the user, they re-send.

403.description: Invite email doesn't match the caller's email

404.description: Invite not found, expired, or no longer PENDING

get.description: Bearer-authenticated. Vercel Cron invokes this endpoint per the schedule in `vercel.json` (currently `30 3 * * *` — 03:30 UTC). Four ordered steps in one run: (1) expired invites, (2) sessions older than `SESSION_RETENTION_DAYS = 90`, (3) tracked users with zero remaining sessions, (4) organisations with zero memberships (after nullifying every `User.activeOrganizationId` that referenced them). The `GET` verb is Vercel Cron's contract — destructive-on-GET is ugly but the alternative is a wrapper that fights the platform.

parameters.name: authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token matching `CRON_SECRET`. Vercel injects this on scheduled invocations.

200.description: Cleanup summary — counters per entity removed
